element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn collision avoidance and tile-flipping mechanics against stationary-moving enemies
Core mechanic for propelling enemies and revealing hidden tiles underneath
Variant enemy behavior requiring adapted spatial reasoning
Enemy dolls with same player abilities create dynamic unpredictability and resource competition
Enemies that alter tile states through graffiti, creating permanent obstacles
Obstacles that enable stalemate victories and constraint spatial planning
Collision-based enemy destruction alternative to flipping
Memory and pattern anticipation required; removes visual safety net
Revealed through repeated flips; creates exploration incentive
Secondary objective tracked separately per player; optional scoring
Pressure relief mechanic; extends timer when collected

typical_relief_triggers: Destroying all enemy dolls to complete level
Collecting time-extension tiles to reduce timer pressure
Achieving stalemate state with unflippable tiles
Regaining visibility in dark rooms after pattern memorization
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy doll proximity and collision threat
Aggressive tile-flipping enemies competing for board control
Loss of visibility in darkness zones
Stale board states (few viable moves remaining)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Single-room puzzle-action with escalating enemy complexity per world. Tension builds through enemy density and behavioral unpredictability rather than spatial expansion. Relief comes from discrete level completion rather than extended safe passages.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple hidden tile layers beneath visible surface; encouraged through repeated flipping to discover star patterns and power-ups
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Dark/low-visibility rooms require spatial memory and pattern anticipation; players must learn enemy behavior patterns and navigate by inference

difficulty_curve.early_game: Simple walking enemies in well-lit rooms; basic tile-flipping against stationary or predictable patterns. Teaches core mechanic without spatial or temporal pressure.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of specialized enemy types (swimmers, artists, aggressive flippers) with world-specific behaviors. Dark rooms begin appearing. Boss encounters punctuate each world's completion.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Frequent dark-room levels requiring pattern memory. Multiple aggressive tile-flippers competing for board state. High density of enemy dolls. Minimal safe spaces or timer extensions.
notable_spikes: Introduction of dark/low-visibility rooms (severe spatial disorientation)
First aggressive tile-flipping enemy encounter (loss of mechanic exclusivity)
Boss encounters at world transitions (density and behavioral unpredictability spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: All levels confined to 5×7 grid of floor tiles bounded by walls. No spatial expansion between levels; complexity scales through enemy count, type variation, and tile pattern sophistication.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic level structure across all 10 worlds

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 10 worlds introduces distinct enemy doll behavior (walking, swimming, tile-flipping, drawing). Players learn world-specific spatial and temporal constraints.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: House of Vinci (artist dolls with graffiti mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Concluding level per world features final boss-type encounter with enhanced or hybrid enemy behaviors. Triggers scene transition showing kidnapped girlfriend being moved deeper into realm.
Visibility-reduced levels requiring spatial memorization and pattern anticipation. Tiles and enemies not fully visible; players must recall layout and infer positioning.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late-game darkness zones (specific levels not named in sources)

notable_level_patterns.description: Alternative to destroying all dolls: render board unflippable through bolted tiles or graffiti, creating permanent stalemate. Enables puzzle-like solutions over action-based completion.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Levels with high density of graffiti or bolted tiles
